author: tersec
content:
question, suppose there was a fork a couple epochs prior and some shuffling changes since so two proposer share a slot. how does FOCIL's 2 slot retention work here? to apply each block still requires an IL. so is it that those ILs not on what the client considers a canonical chain must be checked but not kept, and those on the client's canonical chain (which is the usual req/resp criteria) have to be kept?

and if a client reorgs, see above, if it hasn't been keeping the ILs on other forks, it would mean that at least for 1 slot it couldn't return 2 slots retention
